details: The agent ran kubectl-axi triage and then drilled into each namespace with appropriate diagnostic commands. All 11 fault namespaces were identified with correct root-cause categories: fault-crashloop (DB connection refused), fault-oom (OOMKilled, memory limit too low), fault-imagepull (nonexistent image tag), fault-readiness (readiness probe misconfigured to wrong port), fault-configmap (missing ConfigMap app-config), fault-init (init container crashing due to DB migration lock), fault-unschedulable (nodeSelector with no matching node), fault-pvc (nonexistent storage class fast-ssd), fault-endpoints (service selector mismatch app=web-backend-v2 vs actual app=web-backend), fault-rollout (stuck rollout due to nonexistent image in new revision), fault-multicontainer (sidecar log-shipper cannot resolve DNS for logging service). The healthy namespace was not flagged. All diagnoses are supported by the actual command output observed.
